**Matthew Holden Jr.** (September 12, 1931 – January 26, 2025) was an American [political scientist](/wiki/Political_scientist "Political scientist").[[1]](#cite_note-google4621-1)

## Life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Matthew_Holden&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Life and career")]

Holden was born in [Mound Bayou, Mississippi](/wiki/Mound_Bayou,_Mississippi "Mound Bayou, Mississippi") on September 12, 1931.[[2]](#cite_note-obit-2) He attended public school in Mississippi and [Chicago, Illinois](/wiki/Chicago "Chicago"). Holden graduated from the [University of Chicago](/wiki/University_of_Chicago "University of Chicago") in 1950 and received a B.A. degree in political science from [Roosevelt University](/wiki/Roosevelt_University "Roosevelt University") in 1954. He received an M.A. from [Northwestern University](/wiki/Northwestern_University "Northwestern University") in 1956 and his Ph.D. in 1961. Holden served in the [Korean War](/wiki/Korean_War "Korean War") from 1955 to 1957.

Holden taught political science at the [University of Illinois](/wiki/University_of_Illinois "University of Illinois"), [Wayne State University](/wiki/Wayne_State_University "Wayne State University"),
and the [University of Pittsburgh](/wiki/University_of_Pittsburgh "University of Pittsburgh"). He was a professor of Political Science/Public Policy Administration at the [University of Wisconsin–Madison](/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin%E2%80%93Madison "University of Wisconsin–Madison") from 1969 to 1981. He joined the [University of Virginia](/wiki/University_of_Virginia "University of Virginia") faculty in 1981 and became the Henry L. and Grace M. Doherty Professor Emeritus of Politics.

He served on the [Public Service Commission of Wisconsin](/wiki/Public_Service_Commission_of_Wisconsin "Public Service Commission of Wisconsin") from 1975 to 1977 and on the [Federal Energy Regulatory Commission](/wiki/Federal_Energy_Regulatory_Commission "Federal Energy Regulatory Commission") from 1977 to 1981.[[1]](#cite_note-google4621-1) Later in 1977, Holden was elected as a fellow of the [National Academy of Public Administration.](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Public_Administration_(United_States) "National Academy of Public Administration (United States)")[[3]](#cite_note-3) According to his University of Virginia Department of Politics biography, he published articles in political science and other journals including the [Journal of Politics](/wiki/The_Journal_of_Politics "The Journal of Politics") and the [American Political Science Review](/wiki/American_Political_Science_Review "American Political Science Review").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Holden became President of the [American Political Science Association](/wiki/American_Political_Science_Association "American Political Science Association") in 1989. He retired in 2002.

Holden died on January 26, 2025, at the age of 93.[[2]](#cite_note-obit-2)
